note: Add your own categories and questions by following this structure. Each question needs a unique id (use category prefix + number), text, and optional tags.

categories.name: Friendship
categories.description: Questions about bonds, memories, and what friendship means

questions.text: What's the most important quality in a best friend?
What's a memory from our friendship you'd never want to forget?
How did you feel when we first became friends?
What's something you've never told me but always wanted to?
What's the best advice a friend has ever given you?
How do you know when someone is a true friend vs. just an acquaintance?
What's something you always appreciate that friends do for you?
Have you ever had a friendship that changed your life? How?
What's a friendship red flag you've learned to spot?
If we had to describe our friendship in one song, what would it be and why?
What's the kindest thing a friend has done for you?
Do you believe long-distance friendships can stay as strong? Why or why not?
What's something small I do that actually means a lot to you?
Have you ever lost a friendship? What happened?
What does loyalty mean to you in a friendship?

categories.name: Deep Questions
categories.description: Thought-provoking questions about life, meaning, and existence

questions.text: What do you think happens after we die?
If you could know one truth about the universe, what would you ask?
What's the biggest lie society tells us?
Do you believe people can truly change? Have you?
What's something you believe that most people around you don't?
What would you do differently if you knew no one would judge you?
What does happiness actually look like for you — not the ideal, the real version?
Are humans fundamentally good or fundamentally selfish?
What's a belief you held strongly that you've since abandoned?
If your life were a book, what chapter are you in right now?
What scares you more: failure or never trying?
Do you think free will exists, or are we all just following a script?
What's something you've never forgiven yourself for?
What gives your life the most meaning right now?
If you could send one message to your 10-year-old self, what would it say?
What's the hardest lesson life has taught you so far?
Is it better to know a painful truth or live with a comfortable lie?
What does success actually mean to you — not what you say, but what you feel?

categories.name: Fun & Random
categories.description: Silly, unexpected, and chaotic questions to keep things light

questions.text: If you could only eat one food for the rest of your life, what would it be?
What's the weirdest thing you've ever Googled?
If animals could talk, which one would be the rudest?
What's the most useless skill you have that you're secretly proud of?
If you could swap lives with any fictional character for a week, who would it be?
What would your warning label say if you came with one?
What's the most embarrassing song on your playlist?
If you were a villain, what would your evil plan be?
What game show would you absolutely dominate?
If your life had a theme song that played whenever you walked into a room, what would it be?
What's a conspiracy theory you low-key believe?
If you could have any superpower but it was completely useless, what would you pick?
What's the most unhinged thing you've ever said out loud?
What fictional world would you absolutely NOT survive in?
If you were a ghost, where would you haunt?

categories.name: Personal Preferences
categories.description: Reveal each other's tastes, aesthetics, and personal style

questions.text: Morning person or night owl — and which one are you actually, not which one you wish you were?
What's your comfort food on a bad day?
Books, movies, or podcasts — what do you reach for when you need to escape?
What's your ideal Saturday with zero obligations?
Coffee, tea, or neither — and what does that say about you?
What aesthetic or vibe feels most like 'home' to you?
What music do you put on when you're in your feels?
What's a show or movie you've rewatched more than three times?
If you could live in any decade other than now, which one would you pick?
City life, suburbs, or countryside — where do you actually want to end up?
What's your love language when it comes to friendship?
Rainy day indoors or sunny day out — which charges you more?
What's something you spend money on without guilt?
What's a genre of content you'd never admit you love?

categories.name: Would You Rather
categories.description: Impossible choices that reveal everything about who you are

questions.text: Would you rather know how you'll die or know exactly when you'll die?
Would you rather be famous but misunderstood or unknown but deeply loved?
Would you rather lose all your memories or never be able to make new ones?
Would you rather have the ability to read minds or to be invisible?
Would you rather relive the best day of your life or never forget any day ever?
Would you rather have every song you hum instantly play out loud or narrate your life like a documentary?
Would you rather only be able to whisper or only be able to shout?
Would you rather live 200 years in the past or 200 years in the future?
Would you rather always know when someone is lying or always know what they're really thinking?
Would you rather have no internet for a year or no human contact for three months?
Would you rather be feared or be forgotten?
Would you rather have perfect health but be broke or have all the money but bad health?
Would you rather always arrive two hours early or always arrive two hours late?
Would you rather speak every language fluently or play every instrument perfectly?

categories.name: Hypothetical Scenarios
categories.description: What if situations that reveal your values and instincts

questions.text: If you woke up as the opposite gender for one week, what's the first thing you'd do?
If you could teleport anywhere instantly right now, where would you go and why?
If you had to delete all social media permanently, how would your life change?
If you found out your entire life was a simulation, how would you react?
If you had one year to live and unlimited money, how would you spend it?
If you could go back and change one decision in your life, what would it be?
If you had to live in another country for the rest of your life, where would you go?
If you could only save three things from your home in a fire, what would they be?
If you were given 24 hours of absolute invisibility, what would you actually do?
If a genie could grant you one non-material wish, what would it be?
If you could master any skill overnight, what would you choose?
If you had to start completely over — new name, city, life — what would you change?
If you could read anyone's diary, whose would it be and why?
If the internet permanently shut down tomorrow, what would you miss most?

categories.name: Culture & Language
categories.description: Explore identity, heritage, language, and how culture shapes us

questions.text: What's a tradition from your culture that you genuinely love?
What's a word or phrase from your language that has no perfect translation?
What part of your cultural identity do you feel most proud of?
What's something about your culture that you think others misunderstand?
Has living online ever made you feel more connected to a culture you weren't born into?
What food from your culture do you wish more people knew about?
What's a stereotype about your country that's actually kind of true?
Is there a part of your cultural background you've had to unlearn?
What's a piece of media (show, song, book) from your culture that shaped you?
Do you think the internet is creating one global culture? Is that good or bad?
What language would you learn next if you could do it instantly?
What cultural value do you hold that sometimes clashes with the world around you?

categories.name: Future Goals
categories.description: Dreams, ambitions, and where you're heading

questions.text: Where do you see yourself in 5 years — honestly, not optimistically?
What's a dream you have that you've been too scared to say out loud?
What's something you want to build, create, or leave behind in this world?
What does your ideal version of the next 12 months look like?
What's one thing holding you back from something you actually want?
If money weren't a factor, what would you spend your days doing?
What's a goal you gave up on that you still think about?
What kind of person do you hope to become?
What's something you want to try before you turn 30 (or 40, or whatever's next)?
If you had to pick one place to live for the next 10 years, where would it be?
What's a skill you're actively trying to build right now?
How do you want people to describe you in 10 years?

categories.name: Relationship-Friendly
categories.description: Questions about connection, care, and closeness — platonic version

questions.text: What's the most important thing you need from people you're close to?
How do you show people you care about them without saying it directly?
What's something small someone does that makes you feel truly seen?
What does it take for someone to earn your full trust?
When do you need space, and how do you communicate that?
What's a habit of yours that the people close to you probably find annoying?
How do you act when you're going through something hard but don't want to talk about it?
What's your biggest fear about letting people in?
What does a truly healthy relationship (any kind) look like to you?
What's something you wish people understood about how you communicate?
What's an expectation you have of close people that you've never actually said?
When was the last time you felt genuinely understood by someone?

categories.name: Adult
categories.description: Mature questions for adults only — not explicit, but unfiltered

questions.text: What's the most morally questionable thing you've done that you don't regret?
What's a boundary you've crossed that you wish you hadn't?
What's the wildest thing you've actually done that no one believes when you tell them?
What's something you've kept secret from everyone in your life?
Have you ever wanted something you knew was bad for you and pursued it anyway?
What's a desire or craving you'd be embarrassed if people found out about?
What's the most reckless decision you've ever made?
Have you ever lied to protect yourself in a situation where honesty would have been better?
What's a habit or vice you know you should quit but haven't?
What's the most extreme opinion you have that you'd only say anonymously?
Is there something about yourself you've never admitted to anyone?
What's the most complicated feeling you've ever had about someone?
